title: AppShell — Desktop + Mobile OS Shell
description: Generic, brand-free OS-style shell framework. One <AppShell manifest> wrapper provider gives a project a SWITCHABLE multi-shell OS — macOS, Windows 11, Android (Material-You), iOS, plus consumer-registered shells (e.g. a single-pane Dashboard) — over ONE shared window store, driven entirely by a manifest (brand, apps, features, surface regions, capabilities, persistence, keymap). Features (search/⌘K with a dynamic command registry, quick-look, clipboard history, share sheet, shortcut cheat-sheet, lock screen, widgets incl. a desktop wallpaper-layer stack, control-center, inspector, notifications with actions + app badges) plug in via extension points; window manager covers snap halves/quadrants/thirds, always-on-top, virtual desktops (Spaces), window tabs, saved layouts, session profiles, cross-app drag & drop, focus mode and document.title sync. Responsiveness is a single provider + shared primitives. Imports no apps or brand strings — the consumer injects everything.

scenarios.title: Sidebar collapsed
scenarios.description: AppFrame body with the rail hidden — content spans the full pane.

scenarios.title: Sidebar expanded
scenarios.description: Two-column body: a 56-wide sidebar rail beside the content area.

scenarios.title: With toolbar
scenarios.description: AppFrame header + toolbar rows above the sidebar/content body.

previews.seed: Scaled-down shell chrome in a fixed-height card — AppFrame header/toolbar/body composed with a mock sidebar + content list (no window manager, no providers).
